name: Nu'uanu Pali Lookout
tagline: The most dramatic viewpoint on Oahu — sheer cliffs, howling winds, and the entire Windward Coast spread out below.
bestTime: Morning for clearest views. Afternoon clouds roll in. Trade winds blast through here — hold onto hats, phones, and small children
kamaaina: $3 parking for Hawaii residents with valid ID
talkStory: This is where King Kamehameha I won the decisive 1795 Battle of Nu'uanu, driving hundreds of O'ahu warriors over the 1,000-foot cliffs to unify the Hawaiian Islands. In the 1890s, construction crews building the Pali Highway discovered over 800 skulls at the cliff base. The wind here is legendary -- sustained 30-40 mph gusts are normal, and on extreme days it's strong enough to blow car doors shut. Great combined with the Old Pali Road hike that starts nearby.
